particulars.description: Firstly. All that the premises comprised in an indenture of conveyance dated the 16TH october 1880 and made between george doherty (therein described) and samuel wade of the first part james smith and jane anne smith of the second part and the belfast banking company of the third part and therein described as all that and those the dwelling house tenament yard and premises with the rights members privileges and appurtenances there unto belonging or in any ways appertaining as formerly in the actual occupation and possession of the said margaret anne smith lately in the occupation and possession of the said james smith (party hereto) and now in the occupation of mr andrew mckinney as tenant from year to year bounded in the front by the main street of strabane aforesaid in the rere by the river mourne on the upper side by the house and premises occupied by ms sarah kinkead as tenant thereof to john honeyford and on the lower side by the dwelling house in which one richard gwynne formerly resided and which is now occupied by john honeyford and which premises are situate lying and being in the main street of said town of strabane parish of camus barony of strabane and county of tyrone aforesaid.. Secondly. All that the premises comprised in an indenture of conveyance dated the 29TH day of december 1880 and made between john honeyford of the one part and belfast banking company of the other part and therein described as all that and those that house tenament and premises situate lying and being in the town of strabane in the county of tyrone described in the indenture of the 29TH day of march 1870 as "bounded on the south by the tenament of john craig formerly in the possession of john quigley and on the north with the tenement of culbert black and fronting the castle street of the town of strabane containing in length blank feet and extending back to the river mourne with all the backhouses brew houses stables gardens and office house thereon to belonging or in any wise appertaining or deemed taken used occupied possessed and enjoyed as part parcel or member thereof and the rents issues and profits of all and singular the said messuage tenament and premises and every part thereof' which said premises were formerly in the possession of henry mcdougal hatter and are now or lately in the actual occupation of ms kinkead together with their and every of their rights members and appurtenances and all deeds muniments writings and evidences whatsoever relating thereto in the custody or power of the said john honeyford and which said messages hereditaments and premises are situate in the townland of strabane and abutting in front on the main street of the said town in the parish of camus juxta mourne barony of strabane and county of tyrone and are particularly delineated on the map endorsed on the margin of these present and coloured red treated as a deed by the said belfast banking company. Which said premises are now known as 66 main street, strabane, county tyrone.
